skill.name: maxhub-tiktok
skill.description: TikTok data query assistant covering video details, user data, search, ads, creator tools, e-commerce, and interaction across App and Web APIs.
skill.summary: Query TikTok data across 7 modules via the MaxHub API with multi-language support.
skill.author: XieWxx

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 3217 potential issues across 9 files, but after AI evaluation, the vast majority are false positives from API reference documentation. All network URLs point to the legitimate MaxHub API (www.aconfig.cn). All external command findings are curl examples in documentation, not executable code. The MAXHUB_API_KEY environment variable is required for the skill to function and is transparently documented with security best practices. Risk is set to medium because the skill transmits credentials to a third-party API service, and there is a minor discrepancy between the read-only claim in SKILL.md and interaction endpoints listed in README.

medium_findings.title: Third-party API credential transmission
medium_findings.description: The MAXHUB_API_KEY environment variable is transmitted to a third-party API service (www.aconfig.cn) with every API request. This is a legitimate design pattern for API-based skills, but users should be aware that their API credentials are sent to an external service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The credential transmission is clearly documented and expected for this type of API skill. No malicious exfiltration pattern detected.

medium_findings.title: Discrepancy between read-only claim and interaction endpoints
medium_findings.description: SKILL.md claims the skill is read-only, but README.md lists interaction endpoints (like, follow, post_comment) that imply write operations. This inconsistency could lead users to expect write capability that may execute against the third-party API.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The interaction endpoints listed in README do not have the fetch_ prefix, suggesting they are actual write operations. The read-only claim in SKILL.md contradicts this, though no evidence confirms the endpoints are actually called.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs in documentation
low_findings.description: Static analysis found 135 hardcoded URL instances across documentation files. All URLs point to the legitimate MaxHub API (www.aconfig.cn) or TikTok endpoints. These are expected in an API documentation skill and pose no risk.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All URLs are clearly API documentation references in markdown documentation files. No evidence of malicious network targets.

low_findings.title: Shell command examples in documentation
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged 2296 instances of curl commands in markdown files. These are documentation examples showing users how to call the API, not executable code. The commands contain no dynamic user input or injection vectors.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All flagged shell commands are curl examples inside code blocks in markdown documentation files. They use hardcoded arguments or environment variable references with no injection risk.

low_findings.title: API endpoint names flagged as weak cryptography
low_findings.description: References to TTencrypt, encrypt_strdata, decrypt_strdata in API reference files were flagged as weak cryptographic algorithm usage. These are TikTok API endpoint names in documentation, not actual cryptographic implementations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: These are API endpoint names listed in reference documentation tables, not cryptographic function implementations. The names are part of TikTok's API naming convention.

low_findings.title: API endpoint names flagged as C2 keywords
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged endpoint names like fetch_guest_cookie and fetch_web_guest_cookie as C2 keywords. These are TikTok API endpoint names in reference documentation with no actual command-and-control functionality.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: These are TikTok API endpoint names in reference documentation. The static analyzer matched keyword patterns without context.

low_findings.title: High entropy in reference files
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged high entropy in api-user.md, api-video.md, and SKILL.md. This is expected for large API documentation files containing mixed language text (Chinese and English), code examples, URLs, and parameter tables.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reference files are naturally high-entropy due to multi-language content and API documentation structure. No obfuscated or encrypted payloads found.

low_findings.title: API endpoint names flagged as system reconnaissance
low_findings.description: Endpoint names related to device registration, fingerprint generation, and user info retrieval were flagged as reconnaissance. These are legitimate TikTok API endpoints documented for data query purposes, not actual system scanning commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged strings are API endpoint names in reference documentation, not actual system commands or probes.

content.user_title: Query TikTok data across 7 modules with AI
content.value_statement: Marketers, analysts, and creators need fast access to TikTok data without building custom scrapers. This skill lets you query video details, user profiles, search results, ads analytics, creator insights, shop data, and interaction metrics through a single API integration.
actual_capabilities: Fetch TikTok video details, comments, replies, and user posts
Search for videos, users, live streams, and trending keywords
Retrieve TikTok ad analytics, creator account data, and keyword insights
Query creator shop products, product reviews, and live room data
Generate TikTok signatures and tokens (XBogus, msToken, ttwid)
Support both English and Chinese language responses
limitations: Requires a MaxHub API key from www.aconfig.cn to function
Data is from a third-party API and may differ from official TikTok data
Some endpoints may return 403 errors due to platform restrictions
Interaction endpoints (like, follow) may not work as read-only operations

use_cases.title: Cross-border marketing product research
use_cases.description: Analyze trending TikTok products across regions, compare best-selling items, and generate weekly product recommendations to shorten selection cycles.

use_cases.title: Creator partnership evaluation
use_cases.description: Evaluate TikTok creator influence, audience quality, and content performance before committing to paid partnerships or sponsorship deals.

use_cases.title: Multi-region content trend comparison
use_cases.description: Compare content trends across US, Japan, and Southeast Asia markets to develop region-specific content strategies backed by data.

prompt_templates.title: Quick video lookup
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner user wants to find details about a specific TikTok video
prompt_templates.prompt: Find details for TikTok video with URL https://www.tiktok.com/@user/video/123456789

prompt_templates.title: Creator profile analysis
prompt_templates.scenario: Marketing analyst evaluating a TikTok creator
prompt_templates.prompt: Get detailed profile information for TikTok user @example_user including follower count, engagement rate, and recent post performance

prompt_templates.title: Trending hashtag research
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for trending hashtags related to skincare on TikTok and show the top 10 with post counts and engagement metrics

prompt_templates.title: Multi-endpoint deep analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze TikTok creator @top_creator_brand: get user info, fetch their top 20 posts, retrieve creator analytics, and compare engagement rates across the last 30 days

output_examples.input: Show me the top 10 trending videos in the US
output_examples.output:
Here are the top 10 trending TikTok videos in the US:

1. Video by @creator1 - 12.5M views, 890K likes, posted 2h ago
2. Video by @creator2 - 8.3M views, 1.2M likes, posted 4h ago
3. Video by @creator3 - 6.7M views, 450K likes, posted 6h ago

Key findings: Dance and comedy content dominates today's trending feed.

output_examples.input: Analyze the performance of TikTok user @fashion_brand_official
output_examples.output:
**Creator Analysis for @fashion_brand_official**

- Followers: 2.3M (+12% this month)
- Total posts: 1,247
- Avg engagement rate: 4.8%
- Top post: 890K views, 124K likes
- Audience: 65% female, mostly 18-34 age group

**Key findings:** Strong growth in followers. Best-performing content is outfit-of-the-day style videos.

best_practices: Use specific search terms and filters to get more relevant results from the API
Combine multiple endpoints (user info + posts + analytics) for a complete creator analysis
Start with simple queries first to confirm your API key works before running complex multi-endpoint analyses
anti_patterns: Do not share your MAXHUB_API_KEY or include it in any output or shared prompts
Do not use this skill to scrape or bulk-download user data without proper authorization
Do not provide TikTok session cookies unless you fully trust the service provider

faq.question: Do I need a MaxHub API key to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. You must sign up at www.aconfig.cn to get an API key. Set it as the MAXHUB_API_KEY environment variable.

faq.question: Is this skill officially from TikTok?
faq.answer: No. This skill uses a third-party API (MaxHub) to query TikTok data. Data accuracy may vary.

faq.question: Can this skill post or interact on TikTok?
faq.answer: The README lists interaction endpoints, but the skill claims to be read-only. Posting actions may not work.

faq.question: Which languages does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill supports both English and Chinese. Responses match the language of your query.

faq.question: What types of data can I query?
faq.answer: Video details and comments, user profiles, search results, ad analytics, creator insights, shop products, and interaction metrics.

faq.question: Is my API key safe when using this skill?
faq.answer: Your API key is transmitted to the MaxHub API (www.aconfig.cn) with each request. The skill documentation states the provider does not store credentials beyond each request.
